relevant_artifacts.reason: Target table for the four new auto-sync columns (auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping, auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id, auto_sync_run_time, last_auto_synced_at) plus a new household timezone field; existing @auto_init pattern accepts column additions without constructor changes.
Pydantic preference DTOs; new auto_sync_* fields propagate automatically through the Create/Save/Read subclasses once added on Update.

Group-scoped Food model where the new is_pantry_staple boolean column lives; the existing deprecated on_hand column at line 192 is the architectural precedent (a per-household variant exists via households_to_ingredient_foods).

ShoppingListItem (food_id 78, unit_id 75, checked 65) and ShoppingList (group_id 151, household_id via assoc proxy 153) are the merge target tables; household scoping on items/lists is via association_proxy through user, the multi-tenant boundary tests must validate.

All persisted datetimes use NaiveDateTime; the new last_auto_synced_at column must use this TypeDecorator. Docstring explicitly states Mealie uses naive datetimes because the app handles timezones explicitly.

Already implements household-scoped 'today' with timezone-aware datetime.now(tz=tz).date() at line 16; case-5 scheduler must pass tz=ZoneInfo(household_tz) explicitly. Raises on missing household_id, so repo must be constructed with household_id.

Existing PUT /preferences endpoint (line 58) uses can_manage_household() (line 60) as the household-admin auth gate; case-5 must add either PATCH alongside PUT or new admin endpoints under this controller (line 16 router prefix is /households).

Three buckets exist (MINUTES_DAY=1440, MINUTES_5=5, MINUTES_HOUR=60 at lines 15-17); run_minutely actually fires every 5 minutes (line 77). No 30-minute bucket — case-5 must reuse register_minutely and gate internally.

Static class with _daily/_hourly/_minutely callable lists (lines 13-15) and register_* methods (lines 23-48); case-5 wires its task by calling SchedulerRegistry.register_minutely(auto_sync_shopping_to_meal_plan) inside app.py start_scheduler.

EventTypes enum (lines 13-60) requires a new meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping member; docstring at lines 14-22 warns each addition needs a paired database migration on the subscriber notifier-options table. New EventMealPlanAutoSyncedToShoppingData payload class subclasses EventDocumentDataBase.

Public dispatch signature at lines 66-96 takes (integration_id, group_id, household_id, event_type, document_data, message); case-5 task constructs EventBusService(session=session) and calls dispatch synchronously (no bg).
Household-admin auth gate at lines 23-26; new run-now route and PATCH preferences route both call self.checks.can_manage_household() per the spec's 'household admin role' requirement.

Closest existing template for case-5: walks group->household, uses repos.meals.get_today (line 37), constructs household-scoped repos (line 28), dispatches EventBusService events (lines 93-103). Case-5 mirrors this structure but replaces tzlocal() (line 36) with per-household ZoneInfo.

The actual reusable merge surface (the spec's 'consolidate_ingredients' does not exist by that name); add_recipe_ingredients_to_list (lines 413-455) is the highest-level append-with-merge entry point and already handles the (food_id, unit_id) dedupe-against-existing-unchecked-items behavior the spec requires.

Re-exports scheduler tasks; new auto_sync_shopping_to_meal_plan must be added here and listed in __all__. Docstring at lines 21-28 explicitly warns scheduler is 'only available to a single worker', directly contradicting the spec's multi-replica safety requirement — must be addressed via LastAutoSyncedAt CAS.

start_scheduler at lines 124-144 is where each task is registered with SchedulerRegistry; the new auto_sync_shopping_to_meal_plan callable must be added to a register_minutely(...) invocation here for the scheduler to fire it.

Most recent precedent (PR #7431, d2b0681d) for adding new boolean columns to household_preferences using op.batch_alter_table + server_default=sa.true(); exact pattern for case-5's auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping (server_default sa.false()), auto_sync_run_time, etc.

Household model has NO timezone column today (gap noted across all 4 perspectives); preferences relationship (lines 43-49) is cascade='all, delete-orphan' uselist=False so the scheduler can safely load household.preferences.auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping without nullability concerns.

consolidated_artifacts.reason: Target table for adding auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping, auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id, auto_sync_run_time, last_auto_synced_at, and timezone columns. @auto_init at line 42 means simply adding mapped_column declarations is sufficient.
Pydantic DTOs that propagate new auto_sync_* fields to Create/Save/Read via subclassing; loader_options at lines 36-40 already joins Household.group_id.

IngredientFoodModel is group-scoped (group_id at line 158); the new is_pantry_staple boolean column lives here. Deprecated on_hand at line 192 demonstrates the column-pattern precedent (Boolean default=False); however, PR #4616 moved on_hand to households_to_ingredient_foods because it was actually per-household — case-5 must explicitly decide scope.

ShoppingListItem at lines 51-98 (food_id 78, unit_id 75, checked 65, recipe_references 87-89) and ShoppingList at lines 147-181 (group_id 151, household_id via assoc_proxy 153) define the auto-sync target tables; recipe_references linkage is auto-populated by add_recipe_ingredients_to_list.
last_auto_synced_at MUST use the NaiveDateTime TypeDecorator. Docstring at lines 22-27 makes Mealie's UTC-naive convention explicit.

Reuse exactly as-is; the new auto-sync task calls repos.meals.get_today(tz=ZoneInfo(household_tz)) instead of inventing a date filter. Line 14 raises if household_id is unset, so the household-scoped repo is mandatory.
Existing PUT /preferences at line 58 uses can_manage_household() (line 60); the new PATCH route and the new run-now route extend this controller and reuse the same auth gate.
MINUTES_5 at line 16 means run_minutely fires every 5 minutes (decorator at line 77). No 30-minute bucket — case-5 must register on minutely and self-gate via window + LastAutoSyncedAt.
Static class with _minutely callable list (line 15); SchedulerRegistry.register_minutely (lines 41-43) is the new task's registration entry point. Call site is mealie/app.py:134-136.

Re-export module; the new auto_sync_shopping_to_meal_plan must be added to imports (lines 1-8) and __all__ (lines 10-19). Docstring at lines 21-28 calls out single-worker scheduler — drives the LastAutoSyncedAt CAS requirement.

start_scheduler at lines 124-144 wires every registered scheduler task; case-5 must add tasks.auto_sync_shopping_to_meal_plan to SchedulerRegistry.register_minutely(...) (currently lines 134-136 register only post_group_webhooks).

New EventTypes.meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping enum value must be added (lines 13-60); paired migration required per the in-file docstring warning at lines 14-22. New payload class EventMealPlanAutoSyncedToShoppingData mirrors EventShoppingListData (lines 130-132).

dispatch (lines 66-96) is the only public publish entrypoint; the scheduler task constructs EventBusService(session=session) (lines 46-52) and calls dispatch with explicit household_id (no fan-out, per multi-tenant scoping).

Reusable consolidation surface. add_recipe_ingredients_to_list (lines 413-455) appends recipe items with merge-by-(food_id, unit_id) into existing unchecked items; case-5 calls this per mealplan entry, with pantry-staple filter applied to the input recipe_ingredients before invocation.

GroupMealPlan rows are the source of 'today's recipes'; recipe_id FK at line 70 + recipe relationship at lines 71-73. household_id is an association_proxy through user (line 65) — repos.meals.get_today already joins this correctly.
can_manage_household at lines 23-26 raises 403 if user.can_manage_household is false; both the new PATCH preferences route and the new run-now route call self.checks.can_manage_household().

BaseUserController at lines 132-172 supplies the scoped repos/group_id/household_id properties; BaseAdminController at lines 175-189 broadens to unscoped repos. The new run-now controller inherits BaseUserController for household-scoped data access.

Dependency-injection contract for the auto-sync task; ingredient_foods (lines 139-141) is group-scoped only — confirms is_pantry_staple is per-group when stored on IngredientFoodModel; household_preferences (lines 244-253) is the repo case-5 uses for the CAS update of last_auto_synced_at.

Household model lacks any timezone column (gap), but preferences relationship (lines 43-49) guarantees a HouseholdPreferences row exists per Household so the scheduler can read auto_sync_* fields unconditionally.

Migration template for case-5: op.batch_alter_table('household_preferences') + add_column with server_default. PR #7431 d2b0681d is the most recent precedent for adding columns to household_preferences.

Existing foods CRUD; update_one at lines 69-73 uses can_organize() gate. New is_pantry_staple field is read/written through CreateIngredientFood schema (which it extends) so update_one accepts it without route changes; spec says admin gate which is can_manage_household per spec wording.

i18n target; new auto-sync.* keys (auto-sync.no-meal-plan-today, auto-sync.no-target-list, auto-sync.already-synced-today) added under a new top-level 'auto-sync' object. en-US only per project convention — other locales managed by Crowdin.

conflicts.description: is_pantry_staple scope: data perspective accepts spec-literal column on IngredientFoodModel (group-scoped); history perspective flags this as repeating the on_hand mistake corrected by PR #4616 (e9892aba), which moved on_hand from a column to households_to_ingredient_foods because foods are group-scoped but the flag was per-household. Spec test requires 'cross-household pantry-staple does not interfere' which is satisfied trivially by group-scoping ONLY when households-in-the-same-group are not expected to differ.
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Escalate as BlockingDecision NC-001: implement spec-literal group-scoped column with server_default=false; if reviewer rejects, fall back to households_to_pantry_staple_foods association table mirroring the on_hand pattern.

conflicts.description: Scheduler 30-minute cadence: api perspective recommends adding a new MINUTES_30 bucket; test perspective recommends staying inside register_minutely with internal window gating to honor the spec's 'must reuse existing scheduler abstractions' constraint. Both work; choosing one affects test pattern (one invocation vs N).
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Adopt register_minutely + internal gate; cheaper to test (mirrors post_webhooks last_ran precedent) and matches the explicit reuse constraint in the input.

conflicts.description: Per-household timezone storage: data perspective proposes adding HouseholdPreferencesModel.timezone (IANA name); test perspective notes no test pattern exists and proposes parameterizing over UTC/NY/Tokyo. No conflict on the data shape, but conflict on default: data says fall back to server-local then UTC; test says default to UTC explicitly to avoid hidden host-tz coupling.
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Add HouseholdPreferencesModel.timezone as nullable String IANA name with server_default NULL; runtime fallback is ZoneInfo(prefs.timezone) when set, else ZoneInfo('UTC'). Server-local is intentionally NOT in the fallback chain to keep behavior deterministic across deployments.

conflicts.description: PATCH vs PUT for /api/households/preferences: api perspective notes only PUT exists today (controller_household_self_service.py:58) and proposes adding a real PATCH with exclude_unset partial semantics; history perspective notes the project has actively removed boolean prefs flags (#5684) and would scrutinize new add-only PATCH semantics. Both agree extending UpdateHouseholdPreferences with the three new fields is mandatory regardless.
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Extend UpdateHouseholdPreferences with new fields (mandatory); ALSO add PATCH /api/households/preferences accepting a partial AutoSyncPreferencesPatch body with exclude_unset semantics, so clients that only want to toggle auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping do not need to round-trip the whole preferences object.

conflicts.description: EventTypes enum migration: api perspective flags the docstring requirement (event_types.py:14-22) that new enum members need a matching boolean column on group_events_notifier_options; history perspective confirms via PR #7015 (e52a887e) that this is the established pattern (cdc93edaf73d migration). Risk: easy to forget, breaks fresh-DB integration tests but passes unit tests.
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Include a single alembic migration that does BOTH the household_preferences column additions AND the group_events_notifier_options column addition for the new event type, plus the ingredient_foods.is_pantry_staple column. Single migration keeps the up/down deterministic.

consolidated_conventions: Mealie uses naive UTC datetimes via NaiveDateTime TypeDecorator; never use sa.DateTime(timezone=True).
Repositories are group/household scoped through AllRepositories factory; the auto-sync task constructs household-scoped repos per household via get_repositories(session, group_id=g, household_id=h).
Schemas under mealie/schema/ use UpdateXxx / CreateXxx / SaveXxx / ReadXxx suffixes; new field added on Update propagates via subclassing.
i18n only modify en-US.json; other locales managed by Crowdin.
After schema changes run task dev:generate to update TypeScript types + schema exports.
Run task py:migrate -- "description" to create alembic migrations; use op.batch_alter_table for cross-dialect compatibility (SQLite + PostgreSQL).
Scheduler is in-process single-worker; tasks must self-gate via DB CAS (e.g. UPDATE WHERE last_auto_synced_at < today_local) for multi-replica safety.
Event bus dispatch from a scheduled task uses EventBusService(session=session) with DEFAULT_INTEGRATION_ID and an explicit household_id (no fan-out).
Adding a new EventTypes enum value requires a paired migration adding a boolean column to group_events_notifier_options.
Controllers inherit BaseUserController (or BaseAdminController); household-admin checks use self.checks.can_manage_household().
summary: Case-5 introduces an opt-in per-household scheduled job that, once per household-local day, appends today's MealPlan recipe ingredients (with pantry-staple filter) to the household's configured shopping list and dispatches a MealPlanAutoSyncedToShopping event. Reuses repos.meals.get_today (passing per-household ZoneInfo), ShoppingListService.add_recipe_ingredients_to_list, EventBusService.dispatch, and SchedulerRegistry.register_minutely with internal gating. Adds 4 new HouseholdPreferences columns + Food.is_pantry_staple + new EventTypes member + 3 en-US i18n keys, with multi-replica safety via LastAutoSyncedAt CAS. Five cross-perspective conflicts surface: is_pantry_staple scope, 30-min scheduler cadence, timezone storage, PATCH vs PUT, and EventTypes migration coupling.
